name: Money Johnson
abstract: Harold "Money" Johnson (February 23, 1918 – March 28, 1978) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Duke Ellington and His Orchestra and the Terumasa Hino Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Excellent musicians-- Money Johnson, playing [(prose)] trumpet. [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. [INTERVIEWER] Lee Polk. Lee Anderson. All these guys in the band, man.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] There are people like Doc Cheatham and Money Johnson. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yes, I know. Yes, it gets scarce.

about_quotes.speaker: Cootie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now, they said that -- they said Money Johnson was in your band. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Money Johnson.

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: he had Money Johnson sometimes... The weight of the band most of the time was on the drummer and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: George Gavotte played Money Johnson, a Black hustler and a pimp uptown.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, Mr. Chambers, Money Johnson, and me. That was the quintet.
